summary.verdict_desc: 本周计数 5 红 / 7 黄 / 11 绿（上周 5/6/12），red_pct 维持 21.7%、加权风险分由 34.8% 回升至 37.0%——唯一状态变化是 SPY vs RSP 6 月收益差由 -3.5% 翻正至约 +6%（绿→黄）：NVDA 财报后大盘虽广度仍强（S5FI 71%），但市值加权重新跑赢等权重，头部独涨苗头初现。基础判读本在观察期，但分类强制升级再次生效：估值 2/3 红（CAPE 41.6、Top-5 ≈27.5%）叠加资金面 3/4 红（Mag4 FCF 深度负值、VC AI 占比 80%、NVDA 客户投资/收入 43%）。本周关键事件：Cerebras IPO 完成（首日 +68%、募资 $5.6B），SpaceX 路演 6/4 启动、目标 $1.75T；OpenRouter 月 token 量突破 100 万亿（半年 5 倍）、估值翻倍至 $1.3B；Anthropic ARR 升至 $45B；Warsh 5/22 正式接任 Fed 主席，6 月会议预计移除宽松倾向、转向偏鹰。估值 + 资金面双类共振触发分类强制升级。
summary.verdict_desc_en: This week prints 5 red / 7 yellow / 11 green (vs 5/6/12), red_pct holds at 21.7% while the weighted risk score ticked back up to 37.0% from 34.8%. The one status change: the SPY-vs-RSP 6-month spread swung from -3.5% to roughly +6% (green to yellow). Breadth is still healthy (S5FI 71%) post-NVDA, but cap-weighted is once again outrunning equal-weighted — the first hint of a top-heavy regime returning. The base framework would read Observation, but the category-override fires again: valuation 2/3 RED (CAPE 41.6, Top-5 ≈27.5%) plus capital 3/4 RED (Mag4 FCF deeply negative, VC AI share 80%, NVDA customer-financing 43%). Key events: Cerebras completed its IPO (+68% day one, $5.6B raised) and SpaceX kicks off its roadshow June 4 at a $1.75T target; OpenRouter crossed 100T tokens/month (5x in six months) and doubled its valuation to $1.3B; Anthropic ARR reached $45B; Kevin Warsh took the Fed chair on May 22 and the June meeting is expected to drop the easing bias and tilt hawkish. Valuation + capital dual-category resonance triggers the forced upgrade.

wow_changes.note: 6 月收益差由 -3.5% 翻正至约 +6%：SPY 3 月领先 RSP 约 8pp（9.7% vs 1.7%），市值加权重新跑赢等权重，头部独涨苗头初现，越过 5% 黄线
wow_changes.note_en: The 6-month spread flipped from -3.5% to about +6%: SPY now leads RSP by roughly 8pp over three months (9.7% vs 1.7%), cap-weighted is outrunning equal-weighted again — an early top-heavy signal that clears the 5% yellow line

wow_changes.note: Warsh 5/22 正式接任主席，4 月纪要显示内部转鹰；6/17 首场 FOMC 维持概率 99%，但市场预计移除宽松倾向、为潜在加息铺路
wow_changes.note_en: Warsh officially took the chair on May 22 and April minutes show an internal hawkish shift; the June 17 FOMC carries 99% hold odds, but markets expect the easing bias to be removed, paving the way for a possible hike

wow_changes.to: 升温（Cerebras 上市 + SpaceX 路演）
wow_changes.note: Cerebras 5/14 上市首日 +68%、募资 $5.6B；SpaceX 路演 6/4 启动、目标 $1.75T；Anthropic 10 月 $900B、OpenAI Q4——合计 $3T+ 史上最密集 AI IPO 管道，但具体公司数仍 <10 未达洪流
wow_changes.note_en: Cerebras IPO'd on May 14 (+68% day one, $5.6B raised); SpaceX begins its roadshow June 4 at $1.75T; Anthropic eyes October at $900B and OpenAI Q4 — a combined $3T+, the densest AI IPO pipeline ever, yet the count is still under 10, short of a Flood

wow_changes.note: OpenRouter 周 token 量飙至 25 万亿（月 100 万亿、半年 5 倍），估值翻倍至 $1.3B；Google Antigravity 日 token 量 3 月 0.5T → 5 月中旬 3T；全产业用量继续加速
wow_changes.note_en: OpenRouter's weekly tokens surged to 25T (100T/month, 5x in six months), doubling its valuation to $1.3B; Google's Antigravity went from 0.5T/day in March to 3T by mid-May — industry-wide usage keeps accelerating

wow_changes.note: Anthropic ARR 升至 $45B（2025 底 $9B），增速持续领先；OpenAI 约 $33B run rate；二阶导维持正向，但 OpenAI 对 Anthropic 毛收入口径提出约 $8B 争议
wow_changes.note_en: Anthropic ARR reached $45B (from $9B at end-2025), still outgrowing peers; OpenAI is near a $33B run rate; the 2nd derivative stays positive, though OpenAI disputes ~$8B of Anthropic's gross-revenue accounting

indicators.note: CAPE 约 41.6（较上周 42.04 微降），仍是 155 年来第 2 高、距 2000 年峰值 44.19 仅约 6%；earnings yield 仅 ~2.4%，估值持续极度拉伸
indicators.note_en: CAPE sits near 41.6 (down slightly from 42.04), still the 2nd-highest reading in 155 years and only ~6% below the 2000 peak of 44.19; the earnings yield is just ~2.4%, leaving valuations extremely stretched

indicators.note: NVDA 仍居 SPY 权重首位（~7.0%），其后 AAPL ~6.3%、GOOGL ~5%、MSFT ~4.6%、AMZN ~4%；Top-5 合计 ≈27.5%，Mag7 占比 34.8%，集中度仍贴近 2000 年极值
indicators.note_en: NVDA remains the #1 SPY weight (~7.0%), followed by AAPL ~6.3%, GOOGL ~5%, MSFT ~4.6%, AMZN ~4%; Top-5 total ≈27.5% with the Mag7 at 34.8% — concentration still hugging the 2000 extreme

indicators.note: Q1 FY27 超预期后远期 PE 落在 24x 附近（GuruFocus 24.04、StockAnalysis 23.8、Finbox 24.1，区间 21-24.5x）；FY28 EPS 持续上修使倍数维持历史低位
indicators.note_en: After the Q1 FY27 beat, forward PE sits around 24x (GuruFocus 24.04, StockAnalysis 23.8, Finbox 24.1; range 21-24.5x); ongoing FY28 EPS upgrades keep the multiple historically low

indicators.threshold_text: 指引下调=红 / 加速=黄 / 稳健=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Guidance cut = red / Accelerating = yellow / Steady = green
indicators.note: 四大 hyperscaler 2026 capex 合计约 $700-725B（MSFT $190B、AMZN $200B、GOOGL ~$190B、META $125-145B），YoY +77%；含 Oracle 的口径 Morgan Stanley 已上修至 ~$805B，2027 预计 $1.1T——无一下调，继续加速
indicators.note_en: The four hyperscalers guide ~$700-725B of 2026 capex (MSFT $190B, AMZN $200B, GOOGL ~$190B, META $125-145B), +77% YoY; including Oracle, Morgan Stanley lifted the tally to ~$805B with 2027 seen at $1.1T — none cutting, still accelerating

indicators.note: Amazon 尾随 FCF 暴跌 95% 至 $1.2B，2026 全年预计转负约 -$17B（capex $200B）；Mag4 合计 FCF 料降至 2014 年以来最低；唯 MSFT 仍正增长——capex 持续吞噬现金流
indicators.note_en: Amazon's trailing FCF plunged 95% to $1.2B and is projected to turn negative ~-$17B for full-year 2026 on $200B capex; combined Mag4 FCF is set to fall to its lowest since 2014, with only MSFT still growing — capex keeps devouring cash

indicators.name_zh: AI 占 VC 投资比重
indicators.note: Crunchbase/KPMG Q1 2026：全球 VC 创纪录 $297-331B，AI 占 80-81%（$242B）；较 2025 Q1 的 55% 大幅跃升；4 月单月 AI 占 66%，资本继续向 AI 极度集中
indicators.note_en: Crunchbase/KPMG Q1 2026: global VC hit a record $297-331B with AI taking 80-81% ($242B), up sharply from 55% in Q1 2025; April alone ran at 66% — capital remains extremely concentrated in AI

indicators.note: NVDA 累计股权投资承诺 $110B+（含 OpenAI $30B）再加 $15B+ GPU 抵押债务；对 LTM 收入 $253.5B 的比率约 43%——每笔投资捆绑采购协议，循环融资规模仍远超 Lucent 1999 峰值 24%
indicators.note_en: NVDA's cumulative equity commitments top $110B (incl. $30B in OpenAI) plus $15B+ in GPU-backed debt; against $253.5B LTM revenue that is ~43% — every deal bundled with a purchase agreement, circular financing still far above Lucent's 1999 peak of 24%

indicators.note: S5FI 维持在约 71%，周五收盘 71% 个股站上 50 日均线；NVDA 财报后大盘广度仍稳健，远离 40% 红线，技术面中性偏多
indicators.note_en: S5FI holds near 71%, with 71% of SPX names above their 50-day average at Friday's close; breadth stays solid post-NVDA, well clear of the 40% red line, technicals neutral-to-bullish

indicators.name_zh: 市值加权 vs 等权重
indicators.note: 形势反转：SPY 3 月领先 RSP 约 8pp（9.7% vs 1.7%）、YTD 领先约 1.6pp（10.35% vs 8.78%），6 月差由上周 -3.5% 翻正至约 +6%——市值加权重新跑赢，头部独涨苗头初现
indicators.note_en: Regime flip: SPY leads RSP by ~8pp over three months (9.7% vs 1.7%) and ~1.6pp YTD (10.35% vs 8.78%); the 6-month spread swung from -3.5% last week to about +6% — cap-weighted is leading again, an early top-heavy signal

indicators.name_zh: AI 龙头内部人卖买比
indicators.note: NVDA 内部人累计卖出 $3.3B（Huang $2.9B、Puri $148M、Stevens $100M+），18 个月零买入、15 名内部人无一买进；个人层面仍以卖出为主；OpenInsider 聚合页受限沿用 ~8x
indicators.note_en: NVDA insiders have sold $3.3B cumulatively (Huang $2.9B, Puri $148M, Stevens $100M+) with zero purchases in 18 months across 15 insiders; individual-level flows stay sell-heavy; OpenInsider aggregation gated, carrying over ~8x

indicators.threshold_text: 洪流=红 / 升温=黄 / 平静=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Flood = red / Warming = yellow / Quiet = green
indicators.note: Cerebras 5/14 上市首日 +68%、募资 $5.6B（Snowflake 以来最大科技 IPO）；SpaceX 路演 6/4 启动、目标 $1.75T；Anthropic 10 月 $900B、OpenAI Q4——合计 $3T+ 史上最密集 AI IPO 管道，但具体公司数仍 <10 未达洪流
indicators.note_en: Cerebras IPO'd May 14 (+68% day one, $5.6B raised — the biggest US tech IPO since Snowflake); SpaceX starts its roadshow June 4 at $1.75T; Anthropic eyes October at $900B, OpenAI Q4 — a combined $3T+, the densest AI IPO pipeline ever, but the count is still under 10, not yet a Flood

indicators.note: ICE BofA HY OAS 5/25 收于 2.74%（274 bps），较上周 286 bps 收窄 12bp、同比 -19%，远低于长期均值 3.94%；信用市场对 AI capex 激增仍未现压力
indicators.note_en: ICE BofA HY OAS closed at 2.74% (274 bps) on May 25, 12bp tighter than last week's 286 bps and down 19% YoY, far below the 3.94% long-run average; credit markets still show no stress from the AI capex surge

indicators.name_zh: 数据中心 ABS 利差
indicators.threshold_text: 走阔 50bps+ = 红 / 稳定 = 黄 / 收窄 = 绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widening 50bps+ = red / Stable = yellow / Tightening = green
indicators.note: CoreWeave $8.5B 设施获首个投资级评级（A3/A-low），DC 证券化发行由 2025 年 $27B 升至 2026 预计 $30-40B、供给旺盛；但 CoreWeave 4 月 $2.75B 五年期高息债 9.75% 显示高收益段成本仍偏高，市场分层加深
indicators.note_en: CoreWeave's $8.5B facility earned the first investment-grade rating (A3/A-low) and DC securitization supply is rising from $27B in 2025 to a projected $30-40B in 2026; yet CoreWeave's April $2.75B 5-year HY note at 9.75% shows the high-yield tier remains pricey — the market is bifurcating

indicators.note: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda 90 日内无评级下调或违约；S&P 将 CoreWeave 展望上调至正面，CoreWeave 5/18 新增 $3.1B 贷款、产能破 1GW；但 $20B+ 债务与表外租赁仍是潜在风险
indicators.note_en: CoreWeave / Nebius / Crusoe / Lambda — no downgrades or defaults in 90 days; S&P revised CoreWeave's outlook to positive, and CoreWeave added a $3.1B facility on May 18 while passing 1GW of capacity; still, $20B+ debt and off-balance-sheet leases remain latent risks

indicators.name_zh: AI 行业 Token 月度环比
indicators.threshold_text: 收缩=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Contraction = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: OpenRouter 周 token 量飙至 25 万亿（月 100 万亿、半年 5 倍），估值翻倍至 $1.3B；Google Antigravity 日 token 量 3 月 0.5T → 5 月中旬 3T；67% 企业月耗 token 超 10 亿，全产业加速
indicators.note_en: OpenRouter's weekly tokens surged to 25T (100T/month, 5x in six months), doubling its valuation to $1.3B; Google's Antigravity went from 0.5T/day in March to 3T by mid-May; 67% of enterprises now consume 1B+ tokens monthly — industry-wide acceleration

indicators.name_zh: Token 增速 / 收入增速 比值
indicators.note: Anthropic ARR $45B（2025 底 $9B，年化 ~80x），收入增速仍快于 token 量增速（半年 5 倍）；单 token 价值持续上行，付费需求真实，比值维持 <1x
indicators.note_en: Anthropic ARR $45B (from $9B at end-2025, ~80x annualized), with revenue still outpacing token-volume growth (5x in six months); per-token value keeps rising on genuine paid demand, holding the ratio below 1x

indicators.name_zh: AI 收入增速的二阶导
indicators.threshold_text: 减速=红 / 平稳=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Decel = red / Flat = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Anthropic ARR Feb $14B → Apr $30B → 5 月 $45B（2025 底仅 $9B），增速持续领先；OpenAI 约 $33B run rate；二阶导维持正向，唯 OpenAI 对其毛收入口径提出约 $8B 争议
indicators.note_en: Anthropic ARR trajectory: Feb $14B → Apr $30B → May $45B (just $9B at end-2025), still outgrowing peers; OpenAI near a $33B run rate; the 2nd derivative stays positive, though OpenAI disputes ~$8B of Anthropic's gross-revenue accounting

indicators.note: McKinsey Q1 2026：72% 企业至少 1 项 AI workload 生产化（2024 55%、2020 20%）；Deloitte（3,235 名高管）：25% 已将 ≥40% 实验转产、54% 预计 6 个月内达标；深度集成仍在爬坡但整体高位
indicators.note_en: McKinsey Q1 2026: 72% of enterprises run at least one AI workload in production (vs 55% in 2024, 20% in 2020); Deloitte (3,235 leaders): 25% have moved 40%+ of experiments to production and 54% expect to within six months — deep integration still climbing but the headline stays high

indicators.threshold_text: 负增长=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Negative = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Oracle RPO $455B（+359% YoY、云 RPO 近 +500%，签约 OpenAI/xAI/Meta/NVDA/AMD）超越 MSFT；MSFT 商业 RPO $392B（+51%）、Azure +40%；GCP 积压 $460B；合计超 $1.12T——递延需求持续强劲
indicators.note_en: Oracle RPO $455B (+359% YoY, cloud RPO up nearly 500%, signing OpenAI/xAI/Meta/NVDA/AMD) overtook Microsoft; MSFT commercial RPO $392B (+51%), Azure +40%; GCP backlog $460B; combined pipeline tops $1.12T — deferred demand stays robust

indicators.threshold_text: 任何=红 / 调查=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Any confirmed = red / Investigation = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 无主要 AI 龙头正式执法；Burry 继续指控 Mag7 借延长 AI 服务器折旧 2026-28 虚增 $176B 收益；SEC 1 月对某数据情报公司 round-tripping（虚增 27%）提起诉讼、Super Micro 仍受 SEC 调查，但均非核心 AI 龙头
indicators.note_en: No formal enforcement against a major AI leader; Burry keeps alleging Mag7 inflates $176B of 2026-28 earnings via extended server depreciation; the SEC charged a data-intelligence firm for round-tripping (27% inflation) in January and Super Micro remains under SEC scrutiny, but none are core AI bellwethers

indicators.threshold_text: 加息=红 / 通胀压力=黄 / 降息=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Hike = red / Inflation pressure = yellow / Cut = green
indicators.note: Warsh 5/22 正式接任主席，6/17 首场 FOMC，CME 维持概率 99%；4 月纪要显示内部转鹰、通胀超目标+劳动力强劲，市场预计移除宽松倾向、为潜在加息铺路；高利率环境维持
indicators.note_en: Warsh officially took the chair on May 22; his first FOMC is June 17 with CME showing 99% hold odds; April minutes reveal an internal hawkish shift on above-target inflation and a strong labor market, and markets expect the easing bias to be dropped, paving the way for a possible hike — higher-for-longer persists

indicators.name_zh: 资本开支指引市场反应
indicators.threshold_text: 系统性惩罚=红 / 偶发=黄 / 奖励=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Systematic punishment = red / Episodic = yellow / Rewarded = green
indicators.note: Q1 财报季 3/4 被惩罚：META 上调 capex 至 $125-145B 后跌约 6-10%、MSFT 与 AMZN 同步下挫；唯 GOOGL 凭 $185B capex+云增长两日涨近 10% 获奖励——市场要求高 capex 必须配以兑现，3 罚 + 1 奖、距系统性惩罚一步之遥
indicators.note_en: In Q1 earnings, three of four were punished: META fell ~6-10% after lifting capex to $125-145B, with MSFT and AMZN also sliding; only GOOGL was rewarded (up ~10% over two days on $185B capex plus cloud growth) — the market now demands high capex be matched by delivery; 3 punished + 1 rewarded, one step from 'systematic'

indicators.threshold_text: 普遍承认过热=红 / 部分=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widespread acknowledgment = red / Partial = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: 对冲表态持续：Pichai 承认 AI 热潮存在"非理性"、"无公司能免疫"；Dimon"比别人更担心、多数人将亏钱"；Altman"有人会损失天量资金"；频率与强度上升，但仍未达全体一致承认过热
indicators.note_en: Hedging continues: Pichai concedes 'irrationality' in the AI boom and 'no company is immune'; Dimon is 'far more worried' and warns 'most will lose money'; Altman says 'someone will lose a phenomenal amount'; frequency and intensity are rising but still short of universal 'overheated' consensus
